role_title: Bilingual Staff Technical Program Manager, Global Enterprise Fulfillment Platform

location.office_time_required: Flex First — no mandated office time; regular in-person events

level_signals: Title explicitly says 'Staff Technical Program Manager'
Base pay range $202K–$255.5K depending on state, consistent with Staff-level IC
Mentors and onboards TPMs at varying levels

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of post-graduation experience in technology as a Technical Program Manager, Engineering Manager, Product Manager, or similar technical role
Fluent in Spanish (spoken and written) with daily collaboration experience across Spanish-speaking engineering, product, and commercial teams.
Proven track record leading end-to-end, company-wide programs of high complexity and ambiguity across multiple engineering organizations and cross-functional partners.
Demonstrated ability to create program structures and cross-program roadmaps; skilled at building execution plans and operating cadences tailored to program needs.
Deep technical domain knowledge enabling you to influence architectural decisions, de-risk complex technical choices, and guide implementation at org leadership level.
Exceptional communication skills, including building executive-level narratives and presenting to VP and C-suite audiences; adept at tailoring messages by audience and context.
Data-driven decision maker with a strong strategic mindset—able to define paths forward for ambiguous problems and identify new, high-impact problem areas.
Experience driving continuous improvement in processes, metrics, and program design; known for setting a high operational bar that others model.
Bachelor's degree in a technology or engineering discipline.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Master's degree in a technology or engineering discipline.
Experience leading programs with active VP/C-suite engagement where outcomes directly influence company strategy and business results.
History of defining foundational, reusable program frameworks, tools, and processes adopted across large organizations.
Experience with AI/ML programs or demonstrated fluency applying AI to program management and engineering workflows.
Background in marketplace, e-commerce, or logistics technology, with familiarity in fulfillment, picking, delivery orchestration, or last-mile logistics.
Experience leading large-scale platform integration programs and coordinating distributed, multi-country engineering teams (e.g., across Latin America and Europe).

scope.team_size_estimate: 12 TPMs on immediate team within a broader 25-person TPM organization
scope.surface: Global Enterprise Fulfillment Platform — integrating Instaleap acquisition capabilities (fulfillment, picking, delivery orchestration, marketplace) into a unified international offering for retail partners across North America, Latin America, Europe, and the Middle East
scope.business_model: B2B2C SaaS/marketplace — enterprise fulfillment platform licensed to retailers, underpinning Instacart's 4-sided marketplace
primary_metrics_inferred: Zero disruption to ~100 existing international retail partner operations during integration
Cross-sell conversion from combined client base
Platform unification milestones across discovery, gap analysis, and evolution phases
Alignment and velocity across distributed NA and Bogotá engineering teams
core_responsibilities: Lead the strategy, definition, and high-quality execution of company-wide programs with high ambiguity, complexity, risk, and executive visibility
Transform poorly understood problems into well-structured, cross-functional programs; architect scalable execution plans and operating mechanisms
Drive deep technical alignment across Engineering and Product at org leadership level
Generate and maintain cross-program roadmaps; proactively identify dependencies, risks, and tradeoffs
Partner closely with VPs and executive leadership to align program goals to business strategy
Establish and evolve program frameworks, processes, and evaluation criteria as reusable standards
Mentor and onboard TPMs at varying levels
Lead integration of enterprise fulfillment, picking, delivery orchestration, and marketplace capabilities into a cohesive global platform
Serve as connective tissue between Instacart's NA engineering org and Instaleap's Bogotá-based teams
Ensure continuity for nearly 100 existing international retail partners during integration

compensation.equity_form: New hire equity grant plus annual refresh grants

green_flags: Compensation range fully disclosed across US state tiers ($202K–$255.5K base)
Equity (new hire + annual refresh) explicitly mentioned
Clear team context: 12-person immediate TPM team, 25-person broader org — strong peer support structure
Well-defined strategic scope: Instaleap integration with specific geographies and ~100 partner continuity goal
Flex First remote policy with no mandated in-office percentage
Mentorship and thought partnership culture explicitly called out
red_flags: Hard bilingual Spanish requirement significantly narrows candidate pool and may be an absolute filter
Dual fluency requirement appears twice in the JD (once as a standalone bullet, once with more detail) — could indicate elevated importance or editorial redundancy
Location pay differential is significant: CA/NY/CT/NJ candidates earn ~$42K–$42.5K more base than 'all other states' candidates for the same role
No visa sponsorship information provided despite international collaboration scope
High complexity expectations (VP/C-suite, cross-country, acquisition integration) with no stated team headcount reporting to this role — may indicate a high-leverage IC with limited direct authority

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Staff' + comp range + 10+ years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and marketplace language)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from #LI-Remote tag and Flex First policy language)
compensation.base_range_usd min/max (stated as ranges per state tier; $202K–$255.5K represents the full US spread)
ambiguities: Spanish fluency appears as two separate minimum-qualification bullets with slightly different wording ('Fluency in Spanish' standalone, and 'Fluent in Spanish (spoken and written) with daily collaboration experience...'). Both treated as hard requirements; may be editorial duplication.

JD is tagged #LI-Remote but Instacart describes itself as 'Flex First' with regular in-person events. No specific office time percentage is stated for this role. Treated as remote-us with optional in-person events.
Visa sponsorship not addressed; work_auth and visa_sponsorship set to 'unclear'.
